channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Planned London Underground strikes called off following negotiations
item.description: Planned strike action by London Underground drivers, initially scheduled for 19 and 21 May, has been called off following last-minute discussions between the Rail, Maritime and Transport (RMT) union a...

item.title: Amaechi Says Economic Hardship Affects Both Nigerians and Governors Under Tinubu
item.description: Former Rivers State Governor, Rotimi Amaechi, don talk say Nigerians no dey alone suffer under President Bola Tinubu administration, even governors dey complain. Amaechi yarn this one for DCL Hausa pr...

item.title: Saad Edhi and international activists detained by Israeli forces while delivering humanitarian aid to Gaza
item.description: On Monday, Saad Edhi, son of renowned Pakistani humanitarian Faisal Edhi, along with several other members of the Global Sumud Flotilla, were reportedly detained by Israeli forces in the eastern Medit...

item.title: Examining the Rise of Christianity and Nationalist Influences in the UK
item.description: In recent decades, Britain appeared to be moving away from Christianity, with declining church attendance and a more secular society. However, a recent report has suggested that church attendance may ...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for May 19, 2026
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald has published its daily Superquiz for Tuesday, 19 May 2026, offering readers an engaging set of questions to test their knowledge. The Superquiz is complemented by a Mini Cro...

item.title: Debate Over Starlink’s Entry into South Africa Amid BEE and Investment Rules
item.description: South Africa is currently navigating a contentious debate over Starlink’s potential entry into the country. Communications Minister Solly Malatsi has proposed adjusting the country’s Black Economic Em...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard confronts Chinese vessel near Pag-asa Island in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has recently challenged a Chinese research vessel operating near Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea. This incident reflects the ongoing maritime tensions in the...

item.title: Harry Styles brings unity and joy to Amsterdam in intimate world tour performance
item.description: On 17 May 2026, Harry Styles performed at the Johan Cruijff Arena in Amsterdam as part of his 'Together Together' tour, marking the continuation of a record-breaking residency. The concert was a vivid...

item.title: Dr Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma participates in Art of Living Foundation anniversary and founder’s birthday in India
item.description: Former African Union Commission Chairperson, Dr Nkosazana Dlamini-Zuma, recently completed a five-day visit to India, taking part in celebrations at the Art of Living Foundation International Centre i...

item.title: Government Confirms Voting Age Will Remain 18 Amid Debate Over PM Adviser's Remarks
item.description: Federal Information Minister Attaullah Tarar on Monday clarified that the government is not considering any proposal to raise the minimum voting age from 18 to 25. This statement came in response to r...

item.title: Four Italian divers confirmed dead after underwater cave incident in the Maldives
item.description: Rescuers have recovered the bodies of four Italian divers in the Maldives, marking the deadliest diving accident in the Indian Ocean nation’s history. The victims, including a marine biology professor...

item.title: Last-Day Voter Registration Sees Frustrations Due to Early Cutoffs

item.title: Cambridge Researchers Develop LEDs from Electrically Insulating Nanoparticles
item.description: Scientists at the University of Cambridge have developed a new type of LED using materials previously thought impossible to power electrically. The breakthrough centers on lanthanide-doped nanoparticl...

item.title: J'Something draws inspiration from real-life encounters for his upcoming single 'I AM'
item.description: South African musician J'Something is taking a deeply personal approach to songwriting with his upcoming single 'I AM'. The artist recently hit the streets to ask strangers the simple yet profound que...

item.title: Trump shares AI-generated sci-fi memes and political commentary on Truth Social
item.description: Following a three-day summit in China, former President Donald Trump spent the weekend in Washington D.C. golfing and actively posting on his social media platform, Truth Social. Among the posts were ...

item.title: Uganda enacts law requiring approval for foreign-backed policy work
item.description: Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni has signed the 'Protection of Sovereignty' bill into law, a measure aimed at curbing foreign influence in domestic affairs. The legislation requires individuals and o...

item.title: NDMA warns of severe heatwave across multiple Pakistani provinces until May 26
item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a high-level alert as severe heatwave conditions are expected to affect large parts of southern and central Pakistan from May 19 to May 26....

item.title: Clarifying Mandates: Ribadu as NSA and the New Homeland Security Adviser Appointment
item.description: This opinion piece wey Yushau A. Shuaib write dey analyse the recent appointment of retired Major General Famadewa as Adviser on Homeland Security under President Bola Tinubu administration. The write...

item.title: Lana Wood Discusses Her Role as Plenty O'Toole in James Bond's 'Diamonds Are Forever'
item.description: Lana Wood, the American actress and younger sister of Natalie Wood, is remembered for her brief but iconic role as Plenty O'Toole in the 1971 James Bond film 'Diamonds Are Forever.' The character of P...

item.title: Investigation launched after woman and two children found dead in Campbelltown residence
item.description: Tragedy struck a home in Campbelltown, in Sydney’s southwest, when police discovered the bodies of a woman and two children on Monday evening. Emergency services were alerted to the residence on Raymo...
